---
title: "Caped Crusader"
subtitle: "Issue 01: The Signal"
author:
  - name:
      given: "Mickaël"
      family: "CANOUIL"
    degrees: "Ph.D."
    orcid: "0000-0002-3396-4549"
    url: "https://mickael.canouil.fr"
institute: "Data Science Superhero League"
date: today
# The cover slide is auto-tagged `.title-slide .comic-cover` with `data-no-sound`
# (see partials/title-slide.html): the comic-cover plugin bleeds the halftone
# cover to the full viewport and the page-turn plugin stays silent on it.
# The comic-fit plugin auto-scales every slide and accepts optional tuning via
# the reveal config, e.g.:
#   comicFit:
#     enabled: true   # turn auto-scaling on or off
#     minScale: 0.5   # floor before switching to scroll
#     maxScale: 1      # never enlarge content
#     tolerance: 1     # pixels of slack to avoid jitter
# Deck-wide page-turn sound can be muted with `data-comic-sound="false"` on
# <body> (set via an include-in-header partial when desired).
format:
  comic-revealjs:
    output-file: index
---

# Issue 01: The Signal {.section}

A new case lights up the night sky.

## Cover {.panel}

::: {#fig-signal fig-alt="A spotlight beam projecting a bat-like signal into a night sky above a city skyline."}

{{< placeholder 600 400 >}}

Signal projection over the skyline.

:::

The signal in @fig-signal calls our hero into action.

::: {.caption .fragment}
`.panel` slide with a cross-referenced figure.
:::

## Briefing {.panel}

Mission objectives, decoded from dispatch.

- [Locate the source of the broadcast.]{.fragment .comic-pop}
- [Question every witness on the rooftop.]{.fragment .comic-pop}
- [Recover the stolen circuitry before sunrise.]{.fragment .comic-pop}
- [Avoid the press at all costs.]{.fragment .comic-pop}

::: {.caption .fragment}
List items revealed with the `.comic-pop` fragment.
:::

## Detective Board {.panel}

::: {layout-ncol=2 layout-valign="center"}
::: {.callout-note collapse="false" title="What we know"}
The signal repeats every 11 minutes.
The pattern matches no known broadcast.
:::

::: {.callout-tip collapse="false" title="What to try"}
Triangulate from the three nearest towers.
Compare with last week's interference report.
:::

::: {.callout-warning collapse="false" title="What to watch"}
The rooftop access is unstable after midnight.
:::

::: {.callout-important collapse="false" title="What matters"}
Recover the circuitry before the trail goes cold.
:::
:::

::: {.caption .fragment}
Themed callouts: `note`, `tip`, `warning`, and `important`.
:::

## Incident Summary {.panel}

| District | Alerts | Resolved | Priority |
| -------- | -----: | -------: | -------- |
| Old Town |     14 |       11 | Medium   |
| Dockside |      9 |        5 | High     |
| Midtown  |      7 |        7 | Low      |

: Dispatch incident summary. {#tbl-incidents}

Table @tbl-incidents shows where to focus tonight.

> The numbers never lie, but they rarely confess either.
>
> Dispatch logbook, night shift.

::: {.caption .fragment}
Themed table and blockquote inside a `.panel`.
:::

# Issue 02: The Investigation {.section}

## Investigator's Notes {.panel}

::: {.panel-tabset tabset-skip-pdf-clone="true"}

#### Narrative {.active}

[The hero arrives at the rooftop while the city still glows.]{.fragment .halftone-wipe}

[Three witnesses are waiting in the cold.]{.fragment .halftone-wipe}

#### Evidence

| Item            | Source         | Status   |
| --------------- | -------------- | -------- |
| Burnt circuitry | Old Town alley | Bagged.  |
| Audio fragment  | Dispatch tape  | Decoded. |
| Footprint cast  | Rooftop edge   | Pending. |

#### Code

```r
city <- c("Gotham", "Metropolis", "Star City")
paste("Patrol active in", city)
```

:::

::: {.caption}
Interactive `.panel-tabset` opening on tab 2 via `data-tab-active`.
:::

## Witness Statement {.speech}

I saw a flash, and then nothing.

The witness will not say more.

## Inner Monologue

::: {.thought}
If the signal repeats every 11 minutes, the next pulse is in 3.
:::

The hero counts the seconds.

::: {.caption}
A `.thought` bubble div.
:::

## Out of Nowhere... {.action}

ZAP!

{{< bam "POW!" colour=red top=35% right=8% rotate=12 size=2.6em fragment=burst index=1 >}}

{{< bam "WHAM!" colour=blue top=58% left=10% rotate=-8 size=2em fragment=pop index=2 >}}

{{< bam "THWACK!" colour=yellow bottom=12% left=14% rotate=6 size=1.8em fragment=splat index=3 >}}

## "Stop right there!"

::: {.shout}
Stop right there!
:::

The rooftop falls silent.

::: {.caption}
A `.shout` bubble div.
:::

## City At Night {.halftone}

[The neon hum dims below the clouds.]{.fragment .ink-splat}

[Somewhere, a clock tower chimes once.]{.fragment .ink-splat}

::: {.caption}
`.halftone` slide with the `.ink-splat` fragment.
:::

## Showdown {.panel .halftone}

The hero faces the [unknown broadcaster]{.fragment .zap-highlight} on the rooftop.

The signal pulses one more time.

::: {.caption}
`.panel` and `.halftone` combined; `.zap-highlight` fragment.
:::

## {.explosion}

KA-BOOM!

{{< boom "BLAM!" colour=yellow top=62% left=8% rotate=-14 size=1.6em >}}

{{< boom "KRAK!" colour=red top=28% right=12% rotate=10 size=1.5em fragment=burst index=1 >}}

{{< boom "ZRT!" colour=blue bottom=18% right=20% rotate=-6 size=1.2em fragment=pop index=2 >}}

## Aftermath {.panel}

::: {.narration}
Three minutes later, the city is quiet again.
The press is already on its way.
:::

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}

**Heroes.**

- Hero secured the rooftop.
- Witnesses gave their statements.
- Circuitry recovered intact.

:::

::: {.column width="50%"}

**Villains.**

- One escaped into the alleys.
- Identity still unknown.
- Pattern logged for next time.

:::
:::

::: {.caption}
A `.narration` box above two `.columns`.
:::

## "Until next time..." {.speech}

::: {.whisper}
Until next time.
:::

The hero disappears over the rooftop edge.

::: {.caption}
A `.whisper` bubble div on a `.speech` slide.
:::

# Field Manual {.section}

Every device in this issue, labelled for the next writer.

## Bubbles On Demand {.panel}

As a div, `.speech` becomes an inline bubble inside any slide.

::: {.speech}
Use me when a character speaks mid-panel.
:::

::: {.caption}
A `.speech` div bubble, distinct from the `.speech` slide class.
:::

## Handle With Care {.panel}

::: {.callout-caution collapse="false" title="What can go wrong"}
Stacking too many fragments on one slide can outpace the page-turn.
:::

::: {.caption}
The `caution` callout completes the five themed variants.
:::

## Natural Size {.panel data-comic-fit="false"}

This slide carries `data-comic-fit="false"`, so the comic-fit plugin leaves it at its natural size instead of auto-scaling to fit.

Every other slide shrinks, then scrolls, to stay inside the panel.

::: {.caption}
`data-comic-fit="false"` opts a slide out of auto-scaling.
:::

## Silent Panel {.panel data-no-sound=true}

This slide carries `data-no-sound`, so the page-turn plugin makes no whoosh when it appears.

Mute the whole deck instead with `data-comic-sound="false"` on the document body.

::: {.caption}
`data-no-sound` silences the page-turn for one slide.
:::

# Issue 01-02: End of Issue {.section}

To be continued ...

## Evidence Notes and Citations {.panel}

Comic scholarship can still be rigorous.

The signal pattern echoes earlier findings on jointly modelled longitudinal traits.

::: {.caption}
Citations link to the themed bibliography.
:::
